principle: Package/server names are identity, not semantic truth. MCPace may use names only as weak candidate-search evidence; policy decisions require indirect evidence or safe MCP probes.

nameEvidencePolicy.toolName: action-surface-hint-only; combine with description, inputSchema, annotations and transport

indirectEvidenceRanked.whyStrong: proves the process is an MCP server and exposes serverInfo/capabilities/listChanged without calling tools
indirectEvidenceRanked.canRelaxPolicy: only when combined with tools/resources surface

indirectEvidenceRanked.whyStrong: exposes actual tool surface, descriptions, JSON Schemas and annotations without executing tools
indirectEvidenceRanked.canRelaxPolicy: yes for read-only/stateless when schemas and annotations agree; annotations remain untrusted hints

indirectEvidenceRanked.whyStrong: separates runnable server from SDK/example/library and identifies launcher shape
indirectEvidenceRanked.canRelaxPolicy: no; useful for plan-only vs runnable

indirectEvidenceRanked.whyStrong: distinguishes local process, remote HTTP, OAuth/API-token and credential-bound servers

indirectEvidenceRanked.source: manifest dependencies grouped by family, not individual dependency names
indirectEvidenceRanked.whyStrong: playwright/puppeteer/sql drivers/ssh/kubectl SDKs reveal runtime family
indirectEvidenceRanked.canRelaxPolicy: no; dependencies are supporting evidence only

indirectEvidenceRanked.whyStrong: detects data/context surfaces without action tools
indirectEvidenceRanked.canRelaxPolicy: sometimes, if tools are absent or read-only

indirectEvidenceRanked.source: sandbox startup, timeout, stderr class, list_changed notifications
indirectEvidenceRanked.whyStrong: detects unstable, dynamic or host-coupled behavior
indirectEvidenceRanked.canRelaxPolicy: yes downward; upward only with tools/list evidence

indirectEvidenceRanked.whyStrong: semantic clue when it mentions actual operations, auth scopes, required env, examples
indirectEvidenceRanked.canRelaxPolicy: no; text can be stale or misleading

decisionRules: If evidence is name-only, set automaticAction=needs-safe-probe or unknown-conservative.
If package shape says SDK/client/example and there is no runnable bin/command, set plan-only.
If transport is remote HTTP with auth/env/header requirements, default credential-scoped until session behavior is observed.
If tools/list shows only read/get/list/search/query and schemas have no write/delete/update/create/send/deploy-like arguments, allow multi-reader with low maxInFlight first.
If tools/list shows write/delete/update/create/send/deploy/execute or env secrets/tenant/admin scopes, use single-writer or single-session.
If browser/device automation deps or tools show navigate/click/screenshot/evaluate, use host/remote session isolation.
If tool surface changes via listChanged, re-run classification and never widen policy during an active lease without fresh evidence.

random500SecondPassEvidence.finding: Metadata-only is useful for triage, but naming is too noisy. The classifier should treat direct names as search/display identifiers and depend on safe probes plus indirect runtime/package evidence for policy.

minimalAutomaticPipeline: derive package shape, launcher, transport, auth/env hints, dependency families
if weak/conflicting, start server only in probe sandbox and call initialize + tools/list/resources/list/prompts/list
score evidence, pick simple user action, store detailed internal runtimeType/stateClass/effectClass
keep unknown conservative until observed evidence proves safe parallelism
